title: baccharis camporum f. integrifolia
intro: Baccharis camporum f. integrifolia, also known as the sweet broom or Ecuadorian sweet broom, is a plant species belonging to the aster family (Asteraceae). This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic effects. The health benefits of Baccharis camporum f. integrifolia include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and soothe digestive problems. In herbal preparations, the leaves and stems of the plant are often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed orally to harness its medicinal properties.
